commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6a55b3956cd35084080cccc5a7ff68b3e3aad2ac)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves expected subagent completion payloads on retry/expiry give-up by reusing suspended final delivery, adding an `escalated` delivery flag, and adding lifecycle tests.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +60. Total +103 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main has a terminal failed-cleanup fallback, while the PR helper can requeue any expected completion with captured text. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for the success guard, regression coverage, and real behavior proof; automation should not take over while proof is missing.

target.title: fix(subagents): escalate expected completions on delivery give-up instead of dropping them
target.body:
## What On delivery give-up (retry-limit / expiry), an *expected* subagent completion that already produced a result is no longer silently dropped. It is escalated — preserved for user-visible delivery — instead of being cleared. The retry/expiry decision and the existing suspend path are unchanged; only the non-suspendable give-up fallback changes, at both give-up sites. ## Why The give-up handling in `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` has two branches: - **suspend** — preserves the payload (`status: suspended`) so the steering queue can still deliver it to the requester. - **fallback** — calls `clearPendingFinalDelivery` and marks the run `failed`. The fallback runs whenever the completion is not suspendable (e.g. `cleanup: "delete"`, or a non-`COMPLETE` ended reason) even though the subagent *succeeded and produced a result*. In that case the result is dropped with only a log line, so a parent/user that was waiting receives nothing. This is the silent-loss path behind a cluster of open reports. ```mermaid flowchart TD A[completion ready, delivery to parent fails] --> B{retry/expiry exhausted?} B -- no --> R[retry / defer] B -- "yes (give-up)" --> C{suspendable?
ok outcome + cleanup keep} C -- yes --> S[suspend: payload kept, delivered by steering queue] C -- no --> D{expected completion +
captured result?} D -- "yes (this PR)" --> E[escalate: preserve and surface to user
status suspended, escalated flag] D -- no --> X[clear + failed
nothing to deliver] ``` ## Change - New shared helper `escalateExpectedCompletionOnGiveUp`: when a completion was expected and a result was captured, preserve it (reusing the suspend path, tagged `escalated`) instead of clearing the payload. - Applied to **both** give-up sites (`finalizeResumedAnnounceGiveUp` and the resume cleanup handler) so neither drops silently. - Adds an `escalated` flag to `SubagentCompletionDeliveryState`. - Tests for the escalate path and the no-expected-completion regression. ## Scope Deliberately minimal. Does **not** touch the cleanup decision, the suspend path, wake scheduling, session locking, or announce dedup — those are separate concerns in the same cluster and are better handled as their own changes. Core direction for the broader substrate is for the maintainers to steer; this is one contained, safe slice. ## Open question for reviewers The escalation reuses the existing pending-final-delivery path (the steering queue delivers `suspended` entries to `payload.requesterSessionKey`). That stops the silent clear and preserves the result, but final delivery still depends on the requester session being processed. Would you prefer a dedicated root/user escalation notice for the case where the requester chain is fully gone, or is preserving-via-the-existing-path the right first step? ## Validation - `pnpm tsgo:core`, `tsgo:core:test`, `tsgo:test:src` — clean (exit 0, no type errors) - `oxlint` on the changed files — 0 warnings / 0 errors - `vitest run src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` — 64 passed, incl. the new escalate + regression cases Addresses #92433, #67777 Refs #90944, #92116
target.author: mertbasar0
